title: Gorakhnath Math
tagline: Gorakhnath Math — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Gorakhnath Math or Gorakhnath Temple or Shri Gorakhnath Mandir is a Hindu temple of the Nath monastic order group of the Nath tradition.
overview.description: Gorakhnath Math or Gorakhnath Temple or Shri Gorakhnath Mandir is a Hindu temple of the Nath monastic order group of the Nath tradition. The name Gorakhnath derives from the medieval saint, Gorakshanath (c. 11th century CE), a yogi who travelled widely across India and authored a number of texts that form a part of the canon of Natha Sampradaya. The Nath tradition was founded by guru Matsyendranath.

topPlaces.name: Gorakhpur division
topPlaces.description: Gorakhpur division is one of the 18 administrative geographical units (i.e. division) of the northern Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: St. John's Church, Gorakhpur
topPlaces.description: St. John's Church, Gorakhpur is a Protestant church located in the Basharatpur region of Gorakhpur district, Uttar Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Rohni River
topPlaces.description: The Rohni (also known as Rohini or Rohin) is a river which rises in the Chure or Sivalik Hills in Kapilvastu and Rupandehi Districts of Nepal's Lumbini Zone and flows south into Uttar Pradesh state, India. At Gorakhpur it becomes a left bank tributary of West Rapti River, which in turn joins the Ghaghara above Gaura Barhaj, then Ghaghara in turn joins the Ganges.

topPlaces.name: Syed Modi Railway Stadium
topPlaces.description: Syed Modi Railway Stadium is a cricket stadium in Gorakhpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. The ground has hosted two Ranji matches when home team Railways cricket team played against Vidarbha cricket team in 1982.

topPlaces.name: Gorakhpur
topPlaces.description: Gorakhpur (Bhojpuri pronunciation: [ɡoˈɾəkʰ.pʊɾ]) is a city in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh, along the banks of the Rapti river in the Purvanchal region. It is situated 272 kilometres east of the state capital, Lucknow.

topPlaces.name: Vir Bahadur Singh Planetarium, Gorakhpur
topPlaces.description: Vir Bahadur Singh Planetarium (also spelled 'Veer' or 'Bir') is a tourist attraction in Gorakhpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. It has been run by the Council of Science & Technology, Uttar Pradesh since 21 December 2009.

topPlaces.name: Ramgarh Tal Lake
topPlaces.description: Ramgarh Tal is a lake located in Gorakhpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. In 1970, at its largest size, the lake covered an area of 723 hectares (1,790 acres) with a circumference of 18 kilometres (11 mi).

topPlaces.name: Gorakhpur Zoo
topPlaces.description: Shaheed Ashfaq Ullah Khan Zoological Park or Gorakhpur Zoological Garden commonly known as Gorakhpur Zoo is in Gorakhpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. It is named after independence activist Ashfaqulla Khan.

At Gorakhpur it becomes a left bank tributary of West Rapti River, which in turn joins the Ghaghara above Gaura Barhaj, then Ghaghara in turn joins the Ganges.

itinerary.title: Day 2 — Syed Modi Railway Stadium & around

items.note: Syed Modi Railway Stadium is a cricket stadium in Gorakhpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. The ground has hosted two Ranji matches when home team Railways cricket team played against Vidarbha cricket team in 1982.

Gorakhpur (Bhojpuri pronunciation: [ɡoˈɾəkʰ.pʊɾ]) is a city in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh, along the banks of the Rapti river in the Purvanchal region. It is situated 272 kilometres east of the state capital, Lucknow.

Vir Bahadur Singh Planetarium (also spelled 'Veer' or 'Bir') is a tourist attraction in Gorakhpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. It has been run by the Council of Science & Technology, Uttar Pradesh since 21 December 2009.
Ramgarh Tal is a lake located in Gorakhpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. In 1970, at its largest size, the lake covered an area of 723 hectares (1,790 acres) with a circumference of 18 kilometres (11 mi).

Shaheed Ashfaq Ullah Khan Zoological Park or Gorakhpur Zoological Garden commonly known as Gorakhpur Zoo is in Gorakhpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. It is named after independence activist Ashfaqulla Khan.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gorakhnath Math?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gorakhnath Math is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gorakhnath Math?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lal Bahadur Shastri Airport, Varanasi (155 km) and the nearest railway station is Gorakhpur Junction (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gorakhnath Math?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gorakhnath Math at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gorakhnath Math?
faq.a: Stays in Gorakhnath Math start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gorakhnath Math?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gorakhpur division, St. John's Church, Gorakhpur, Rohni River, Syed Modi Railway Stadium, Gorakhpur.
